definition:
Συμεών, ὁ indecl., (Heb. שִׁמְעוֹן), 
 Simeon (so always AV; RV in Luk.2:25, 34 only), Symeon, a Heb. name for which the Gk. Σίμων, which see, was also used; 
   1. the son of Jacob (Gen.29:33): Rev.7:7. 
   2. An ancestor of our Lord: Luk.3:30 
   3. An aged worshipper in the Temple:  Luk.2:25, 34. 
   4. One surnamed Niger: Act.13:1. 
   5. The apostle Peter: Act.15:14, 2Pe.1:1 (R, txt., WH, txt., Σίμων).†
 (AS)
